questions.question:
Choose the word whose underlined part is pronounced differently:
A. before
B. report
C. homework
D. uniform
questions.explanation: The 'or' in homework sounds different than in before, report, and uniform.

questions.question:
Choose the word whose underlined part is pronounced differently:
A. excited
B. annoyed
C. pleased
D. surprised
questions.explanation: The 'ed' in excited is pronounced /ɪd/.

questions.question:
Choose the word that has a different stressed syllable from the rest:
A. classmate
B. finish
C. essay
D. abroad
questions.explanation: Abroad has primary stress on the second syllable.

questions.question:
Choose the word that has a different stressed syllable from the rest:
A. difficult
B. however
C. positive
D. chemistry
questions.explanation: However has primary stress on the second syllable.

questions.question: Sammy studied really hard, so she __________ all of her tests.
questions.explanation: Because she studied hard, she passed her tests.

questions.question:
Student A: How’s the course going?
Student B: I’m __________ enjoying it.
questions.explanation: 'Really' is used to emphasize verbs like enjoy.

questions.question: Ivy was so __________ because she failed two tests last semester.
questions.explanation: Failing tests makes a student upset.

questions.question: We __________ buy lunch in the canteen. There’s a shop next to the school and it sells sandwiches and other snacks.
questions.explanation: It indicates a lack of necessity to buy lunch in the canteen because there's an alternative.

questions.question: The teacher asked us to choose a book from the list, read it at home and then write a __________ about it.
questions.explanation: You write a report about a book you've read.

questions.question: Our math teacher, Mr. Thomas, is funny and friendly, but he gives us too much __________ to do after school.
questions.explanation: Tasks to do after school are called homework.

questions.question: Nick got 100% on his physics test. His twin brother, Adam, ______, had to retake the test.
questions.explanation: However is used as an interjection to contrast Nick's success with Adam's failure.

questions.question: I’m really __________ with Nick. We have an important biology project, but he doesn’t do his part.
questions.explanation: You feel annoyed when your partner doesn't do their part in a project.

questions.question: Yesterday, I wrote a(n) __________ about the benefits of studying overseas, and the teacher said it was good.
questions.explanation: You typically write an essay about a specific topic such as 'benefits of studying overseas'.

questions.question:
Student A: I’m going to the movies this evening. Do you want to join me?
Student B: _________. I have too much homework to do.
options: Thanks, but I don’t think I can.
questions.correctAnswer: Thanks, but I don’t think I can.
questions.explanation: This is a polite way to decline an invitation.

questions.question: Read the notice and choose the correct statement.
options: You have to wear gloves when you enter this place.
You don’t have to wear gloves in this place.
It’s a good idea to wear gloves in this place.
You need to bring gloves to this area.
questions.correctAnswer: You have to wear gloves when you enter this place.
questions.explanation: Usually applied for lab safety procedures.

questions.question: What does this sign mean?
options: You don’t have to talk in this area.
You have to keep silent in this area.
You have to take the exam in this area.
You don’t have to make any noise during the exam.
questions.correctAnswer: You have to keep silent in this area.
questions.explanation: Silent signs indicate obligation to remain quiet.

questions.question: I was so __________ because I got an A plus on my English test. (SURPRISE)
In my English class, we have to give ___________ about various topics. (PRESENT)
Philip was really __________ when he got a D on his math test. He studied very hard for it. (DISAPPOINT)
Studying abroad puts you into an unfamiliar situation, so you will become more __________. (DEPEND)
Studying overseas also gives you the opportunity to make friends with people from __________ backgrounds. (DIFFER)
When I first came to the USA, I was __________ and lonely because I couldn’t understand what other people said. (SCARY)

questions.transcript: Tom: My trip was amazing, although I really missed my friends and family.
questions.question: Tom’s trip was amazing, although he (23)____ missed his friends and family.

questions.transcript: Tom: I was surprised at how welcoming everyone was to me.
questions.question: The thing that (24)____ him was how welcoming everyone was.

questions.transcript: Alex: I’m pleased to hear that.
questions.question: His friend Alex was (25)____ to hear Tom didn’t have trouble making friends.

questions.transcript: Tom: The food was so delicious! Everything there tasted hot and spicy.
questions.question: Tom thought the food was (26)____ delicious because it tasted hot and spicy...

questions.transcript: Alex: How was taking a test in a foreign language?
questions.question: ...and he found it hard to take a (27)____ in a foreign language.
How many years did Ms. Sullivan spend teaching abroad?
questions.explanation: Ms. Sullivan mentions spending four years abroad teaching in Asia.

questions.question: What can an online exchange provider do?
questions.explanation: They can help you make travel arrangements.

questions.question: Why should Simon start saving money?
questions.explanation: He should start saving money just in case something happens.

questions.question: Why would Simon’s mom be pleased with him?
options: because he talked with his teacher
questions.explanation: His mom would be so pleased to learn he is saving money.

questions.question: What is true about the conversation?
options: Simon’s mom was pleased he passed his test.
Simon doesn’t need to save money.
Simon needs advice about studying abroad.
questions.correctAnswer: Simon needs advice about studying abroad.
questions.explanation: Simon approached Ms. Sullivan for advice about studying abroad next year.

questions.passage: Studying abroad is becoming popular for many students looking to learn more about other languages and countries. Sometimes, students (33)__________ work with an online exchange organization to make travel arrangements and make sure they have a wonderful and safe experience while away. Studying abroad is also a (34)__________ way to meet new friends, visit new places, and experience different things. Students are (35)__________ to learn about new opportunities and meet people from other cultures. (36)_________ learning in a foreign country can be difficult, they are pleased when they (37)__________ their first test, complete their first project, or meet amazing new people.
questions.question: (33) A. have   B. have to   C. to have   D. has to

questions.passage: Studying abroad is becoming popular for many students looking to learn more about other languages and countries. Sometimes, students (33)__________ work with an online exchange organization to make travel arrangements and make sure they have a wonderful and safe experience while away. Studying abroad is also a (34)__________ way to meet new friends, visit new places, and experience different things. questions.passage:
When Sophie was a little girl, she dreamed about visiting another country. One day, while looking on the internet, she was surprised to learn about a way to travel and study abroad. First, Sophie would have to decide which country to visit. She would also need to learn a foreign language to take tests and write essays. Sophie quickly knew it would take a lot of work to make her dreams come true, so she began working with her parents on making plans.

Sophie was so excited about her future that she spent an entire summer working with her dad to save money. She also took French lessons because she always dreamed about traveling to Paris. The following summer, Sophie was accepted into an exchange program and spent a year traveling and studying abroad.
questions.question: Sophie made her dreams of traveling abroad come true by working hard. (T/F/NI)
questions.explanation: It mentions she knew it would take a lot of work, and she did it.

questions.question: Rewrite without modifying meaning: Anna’s mother is from China, but she can’t speak Chinese. (HOWEVER)
correctAnswers: Anna’s mother is from China. However, she can’t speak Chinese.
Anna’s mother is from China. She, however, can’t speak Chinese.

questions.question: Rewrite using Although: Luke wants to study overseas, but he is afraid of living alone.
correctAnswers: Although Luke wants to study overseas, he is afraid of living alone.

questions.question:
Rewrite: According to the rules, it’s necessary for students to hand in their assignments on time.
Students...
correctAnswers: Students have to hand in their assignments on time.

questions.question:
Rewrite: It isn’t necessary for Cathy to buy new textbooks because she can borrow them from the library.
Cathy...
correctAnswers: Cathy doesn't have to buy new textbooks because she can borrow them from the library.
Cathy doesn't need to buy new textbooks because she can borrow them from the library.

questions.question: Make a sentence: I / have / be / school / 8:50 / because / first lesson / start / 9:00
correctAnswers: I have to be at school at 8:50 because the first lesson starts at 9:00.

questions.question: Make a sentence: Although / Ann / not like / sports / she / have / play / basketball / P.E. lessons.
correctAnswers: Although Ann doesn't like sports, she has to play basketball in P.E. lessons.

questions.question: Make a sentence: I / pass / all / exams / so / parents / be / so / delighted.
correctAnswers: I passed all my exams, so my parents were so delighted.
I passed all of my exams, so my parents were so delighted.
I passed all exams, so my parents were so delighted.

questions.question: Make a sentence: Tom / play / computer games / right now / although / he / have / important test / tomorrow morning.
correctAnswers: Tom is playing computer games right now although he has an important test tomorrow morning.
